registry_policy.HARD: Do not co-activate under the stated scope.
registry_policy.CONDITIONAL: Co-activation permitted only outside the stated trigger condition.
registry_policy.SOFT: Not strictly forbidden, but co-activation increases risk or complexity.
registry_policy.SEQUENCING_CONFLICT: Can coexist only if staged in different phases or reordered.

edges.rationale: Debridement prunes symbolic scar tissue while EpistemicEscrow depends on scar integrity as a divergence reference.
edges.resolution_strategy: Stage EpistemicEscrow during synthesis and DebridementProtocol only in explicit maintenance windows.

edges.rationale: High mutation pressure and evaluative-language suppression can create contradictory logit pressure in dense contexts.
edges.resolution_strategy: Use one at a time or stage-gate EntropyAnchor before final expressive cleanup.

edges.rationale: Full-session reinjection can leak planner-side context across the isolation boundary maintained by a dual-agent design.
edges.resolution_strategy: Reduce ContextLock scope to task-local or reinject only validated summaries across the boundary.

edges.rationale: Clarification requires a user round-trip, while retrieve-first routing assumes immediate progression into retrieval.
edges.resolution_strategy: Insert ClarifyFirst as Stage 0 before retrieval or switch to a clarify-then-route pipeline.

normalization_notes: HARD and CONDITIONAL edges are the primary operational registry types for automatic validation.
SOFT and SEQUENCING_CONFLICT entries are retained as advisory planning constraints.
Edge directions are treated as symmetric unless the from/to fields encode scoped parameterization.
